def: adj. (f. -ī) = lathaṛ, &c., q.v.

part. adj. Besmeared, bedaubed, draggled, soiled; filthy, dirty, untidy; clogged (with dirt); stiff; viscous, clammy, sticky; — soaked, saturated, drenched; — wallowing, reeking; — lathaṛ-pathaṛ, adj. = lathar (syn. lath-path, lat-pat).
s.f. A staff, stick, club, cudgel, bludgeon; crutch; mace
subs: s.f. Beating with a club or stick; mutual cudgelling, a fight with cudgels ( = laṭhā-laṭhī)
To fight with sticks or clubs, to have a cudgelling match; — lāṭhī ṭek-ke ćalnā or jānā, To walk with the help of a stick
s.m. A man armed with a club or bludgeon, a club-man (such as are in the pay of zamīn-dārs, indigo-planters, and others, and employed to fight when serious affrays take place).

def: s.m. = lāṭhī-wālā, and laṭhiwālā, qq.v.
s.f. A small lāṭhī or stick; a walking-stick, cane.
s.m. = lāṭhī-wālā, q.v.s.v. lāṭhī.

ety: laṭṭhī ( = lāṭhī)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cane, cudgel, belabour.
adj. = lat-pat, and lathaṛ-pathaṛ, q.v.s.v. lat, and lathaṛ respectively.
adj. Saturated, &c. ( = lath-path, and lat-pat, qq.v.).
adj. (f. -ī) = lathaṛ, &c., q.v.

part. adj. Besmeared, bedaubed, draggled, soiled; filthy, dirty, untidy; clogged (with dirt); stiff; viscous, clammy, sticky; — soaked, saturated, drenched; — wallowing, reeking; — lathaṛ-pathaṛ, adj. = lathar (syn. lath-path, lat-pat).
s.f. (colloq.) corr. of lāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. A loose woman, a woman of the town, a harlot; — (dialec.) a tax, an impost
subs: adj. & s.m. Of or sprung from a harlot; bastard, whoreson.

def: adj. Slender, thin, fine, delicate, minute, subtile; occult; — light; — quick, penetrating; — elegant, graceful, genteel, lovely, beautiful; pleasant, agreeable; delicious, exquisite; savoury; — kind, courteous, gentle, affable, benign, benevolent
subs: adj. Of gentle disposition; merry-hearted, &c.; — s.f. A gentle or kind disposition; — a good or fine taste; — a light or merry heart, &c.

ety: for A. لطيفة lat̤īfat, fem. of lat̤īf
def: s.m. A pleasantry, jest, joke, raillery, witticism, bon-mot; an elegant sentiment; — rarity; elegance
subs: or lat̤īfa-go, adj. & s.m. Facetious, witty; — a facetious person, a wit, a jester.

def: s.f. A twining plant, a creeper ( = latā).

ety: lat, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of bad habits, addicted to vicious practices; — a man of bad habits.
v.t. To kick.
s.m. A conjuring rod, a magic wand; — incantation; — a philter; a nostrum; a simple remedy.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Suspended, hanging, dangling, swinging, pendulous

s.m. A hanging or being suspended; anything hanging; an ear-drop; drop (of a lustre or chandelier); a nose-ring; a pendant, an aigulet; a hanging-lamp; clapper (of a bell); a plummet; a pendulum; a stand (for water-pots); — a kind of fruit, Bixa orellana (the pulp surrounding the seeds of which yields a yellow dye); — a kind of handsome green bird, Psittacus minor (so called because it often remains hanging by the claws)
v.t. To cause to hang or dangle; to hang, suspend, swing; to append, to attach (to, -par); — to keep (one) waiting or in suspense; to put off, to defer, postpone
s.m. Suspension, hanging.

ety: laṭak(nā) + auwā = Prk. अअओ (with w inserted) = S. अ+क+कः
def: s.m. A pendant, &c. ( = laṭkan, q.v.).

ety: for A. لطمة lat̤mat, n. of un. fr. lat̤m, inf. n. of لطم 'to slap' (the face
def: s.m. A slap on the cheek; a box on the ear; a slap, blow, buffet, box, cuff.

ety: laṭ, q.v. + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To become tangled or entangled; — to be or become reduced (by sickness), to become lean or emaciated; — to fade away, to lose strength, become weak; to degenerate, deteriorate; to become impoverished, to become poor; — to acquire a habit, or a taste; — (as a gerund) s.m. Becoming reduced; falling off or away; diminution
s.m. The rufous-backed shrike, Lanius erythronotus; the grey, black, and white butcher-bird, Lanius excubitor, or L. lahtora.
s.m. = لٿورا laṭorā, q.v.
s.f. A kind of head-dress.
adj. See s.v. laṭ.
adj. See s.v. laṭ.
v.n. To rock, shake; to be loose or rickety; to totter, reel, stagger; to trip; to walk affectedly, to mince; to tarry, loiter; to stammer or hesitate (in speaking).
s.f. See s.v. laṭ.
adj. Lively, frisky, &c. ( = laṭ-paṭā, q.v.); double-dealing, not straightforward; mischief-making.
adj. & s.m. Rent, torn, in tatters; — a piece, part, fragment, scrap; a fat man, a lazy or inert man.

ety: lat (for latā)+rī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A kind of vetch (syn. dalihan); — an old shoe ( = latar).
s.m. A dirty and tattered cloth; a scrap of cloth, a rag, tatter; clothes (as in kapṛā-lattā).
s.m. = لٿها laṭṭhā, q.v.
s.f. A dialec. form of lat or latā, q.v.
s.m. A beam, rafter, timber; railway-sleeper; a measuring-rod or pole (twenty square laṭṭhās = one bīghā); — a coarse kind of long-cloth (cf. laṭṭhī).
adj. Slack, remiss, slow, lazy.
s.f. A stick, &c. ( = lāṭhī, q.v.); — (dialec.) a piece of cloth forty yards long
subs: s.m. A man armed with a club or bludgeon (many such men are employed by zamīndārs, indigo-planters, and others, with a view to their settling all disputes respecting lands and boundaries).

ety: latt(ānā), Vfr. Wlattā W( = lāt)+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Kicking out (a horse, &c., or a man in swimming)
subs: To kick out; — to take to (one's) heels, to run away.

def: s.f. The string of a spinning-top; a fillet; a cloth tied to the end of a pole to direct the flight of pigeons; the tail of a (paper) kite.

ety: latt˚, fr. lattānā (an unused form fr. lattā = lāt) + ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Kicking (e.g. lattū ghoṛā); — a kicker.
s.m. A spinning-top; — knob, handle (of a door, &c.); a plummet (syn. laṭkan); governor (of a steam-engine); — adj. Enamoured (syn. mohit); dizzy, dazed, dazzled
subs: (-par), To be enamoured (of), to fall in love (with); — to be dizzy or dazzled.

def: s.m. A spinning-top; — knob, handle (of a door, &c.); a plummet (syn. laṭkan); governor (of a steam-engine); — adj. Enamoured (syn. mohit); dizzy, dazed, dazzled
subs: (-par), To be enamoured (of), to fall in love (with); — to be dizzy or dazzled.

def: s.m. = laṭā-paṭā, q.v.s.v. laṭā.

r: laṭūrā, laṭūrī, laṭ, ūrā, ūrī, laṭūre upāṛnā, laṭūre lenā, le-ḍālnā, ke
def: A small curl or lock of hair, a small ringlet; tangled short hair
subs: or laṭūre lenā or le-ḍālnā (-ke), To tear out the locks or curls (of).

def: s.f. = laṭūrī, q.v.

r: laṭūrī, laṭūrā, laṭ, ūrā, ūrī, laṭūre upāṛnā, laṭūre lenā, le-ḍālnā, ke
def: A small curl or lock of hair, a small ringlet; tangled short hair
subs: or laṭūre lenā or le-ḍālnā (-ke), To tear out the locks or curls (of).

def: s.f. = laṭūrī, q.v.
subs: s.f. A woman with short curly hair; a witch.

def: adj. & s.m. Having curly locks; having tangled hair; — a person with such hair; a bugaboo to frighten children.

ety: lā, rt. of lānā, q.v.+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. One who brings, a bringer.

ety: Prk. लोआ; S. लोका, rt. लोक्; cf. laukā, lauknā
def: s.f. Flame (of fire, a candle, &c.), any pointed flame
subs: or lau nikalnā, v.n. Flame to rise, or to break out; to flame, to blaze.

ety: prob. S. लता; cf. S. कर्ण+लता
def: s.f. Lobe (of the ear).

ety: Prk. लओ or लउ = S. लय
def: s.f. Absorption, absorbment of mind; deep meditation; attention; devotion; bent (of mind); ardent desire, longing, craving; frequent and long-continued repetition (of any saying, as of a dying man calling on God, or of a lover on his mistress)
subs: (-se), To fix the attention (on), to be intent (on); — to be constant in prayer, or devotion, &c.
v.n. To be absorbed (in religious meditation, &c.), to be intent(on); — to have a constant desire or craving (for); to make frequent and long-continued repetition (of any saying, &c.)
(see līn), adj. Absorbed (in), intent (on), bent (on); concentrated (on); devoted (to); enamoured (of); rapt (in); eager (for).

def: adj. & s.f. See s.v. lā.
s.m. = laudṛā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A stick, cane, switch.
s.f. A table; a tablet; a plank, a board (especially on which anything is written); — a title-page
subs: s.f. The guarded (or indelible) table (on which, according to Moḥammadan belief, the transactions of mankind have been written by God from all eternity)
s.f. The tablet (on which) and the pen (with which the decrees of the Deity are written).

def: adj. Made of iron; — s.m. Iron.

ety: Prk. लउआलो (with h inserted); S. लय+आलः
def: s.m. A trance.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Younger, junior; — a junior; a cadet.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Younger, junior; — a junior; a cadet.
s.m. What is licked; an electuary (or any medicine) to be licked or sucked, a linctus, a lambative.
s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of pumpion, the bottle-gourd, Cucurbita lagenaria, or Lagenaria vulgaris.
s.m. Lightning, flash, glitter, coruscation.
s.m. (dialec.) A boy, &c. ( = laukṛā, q.v.)
s.m. Logarithm
s.m. A follower of Ćārvaka, a materialist, an atheist.
s.f. The bottle-gourd ( = laukā, q.v.).

adj. (f. -ī), Worldly, mundane, terrestrial; popular, familiar; belonging to ordinary (or every-day) life, ordinary, usual, fashionable, vulgar, common; publicly received, customary; temporal, secular; ceremonial; ceremonious
subs: s.m. The prevailing order of things in religion; customary or fashionable religion
s.m. Worldly wisdom, knowledge or wisdom that pertains to the present world
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Pertaining to worldly wisdom; — possessing worldly wisdom; — a worldly-wise man.

def: s.f. = S لوککتو लौकिकत्व laukikatva, s.m. Worldliness; commonness; worldly currency or custom; popularity; usual custom, usage, general prevalence.
s.m. = S لوککتا लौकिकता laukikatā, s.f. Worldliness; commonness; worldly currency or custom; popularity; usual custom, usage, general prevalence.
v.n. To shine, flash, glitter, lighten.

ety: lok˚; or lauk˚ = Prk. लुक्क(इ); S. लुप्+कृ
def: v.t. To catch (a ball, &c.).
s.m. (dialec.) A boy, lad (esp. an attendant on a goddess)
subs: A goddess and her attendant boy.

def: s.m. A token; a favour.
s.m. See s.v. lava.
adj. See s.v. lau.
s.m. Eager desire, lustfulness, greediness; passion; agitation; — restlessness, instability, inconstancy, fickleness.
s.m. Blaming, accusing, reprehending; blame, reproof.
s.m. Colour, hue; complexion; external form; — species, genus.

ety: Prk. लवणअं; S. लवनीयं, rt. लू
def: v.t. To cut, to reap; — s.m. (gerund) Cutting, reaping; — balancing, making (scales) equal
s.m. An intercalary month
s.m. (f. -ī), A boy, lad; a son; a brat; a page; — a slave; — a catamite
subs: s.f. A woman who goes after young boys.

ety: S. लोष्ट (rt. लू)+कं; cf. loth, loṅdhā
def: s.m. A lump of clay or earth, or of butter, &c.); a clod.
s.m. Boyishness, childishness (syn. laṛakpan).
s.f. A girl; a daughter; — a slave-girl, servant-girl, bondmaid, handmaid
subs: or lauṅḍī-ke peṭ-kā, s.m. One born of a slave-girl; a born slave.

def: s.f. A girl; a daughter; — a slave-girl, servant-girl, bondmaid, handmaid
subs: or lauṅḍī-ke peṭ-kā, s.m. One born of a slave-girl; a born slave.

ety: loṅd = loṅdā + rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र +इका
def: s.f. A small lump, a bit, piece, scrap, &c.

ety: loṅd = loṅdā + rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र +इका
def: s.f. A small lump, a bit, piece, scrap, &c.
s.f. The clove; — a gold nose-pin (having the form of a clove)
subs: or lauṅg-ćaṛā, s.m. A kind of dish prepared from peasemeal (made into balls and iried).

ety: lauṅg, q.v.+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A kind of medicine (made of cloves).
s.f. Sliced (and salted) fruit (as mango, &c., for pickling); pickle.
s.m. = laukṛā, q.v.
s.m. = lauṅḍā, q.v.
s.f. = lauṅḍī and lauṅḍiyā, qq.v.
s.f. = lauṅḍī and lauṅḍiyā, qq.v.

ety: contrac. of lahur = Prk. लहुडी = S. लघु+र+ई
def: s.f. Littleness, smallness (syn. ćhuṭāʼī)
s.m. Membrum virile, penis.
s.m. corr. of launḍā, q.v.
s.m. Contamination, pollution, defilement, impurity.
s.m. Tasting; taste.

ety: Prk. पल्लट्टो; S. पर्यस्त, part. of rt. अस् with परि; cf. palaṭ
def: s.m. Turning over, inverting; turning back, returning.

s.m. Joining, uniting, bringing together (friends); possessing the affections (of), being dear (to); — anything which sticks or adheres; — anything agreeable to the heart; an inclination; an affection; — a cloak; — a man expert and expeditious in business.

ety: caus. of lauṭnā, q.v.; and cf. loṭānā
def: v.t. To turn over, to invert; to turn back; to send back, to return; to give back; to reject
s.m. A clod-hopper, a rude boor, a bumpkin; a lout, a noodle (syn. gabhrū).
s.f. (Braj) = lothī, q.v. (and cf. lāṭhī).

ety: lauṭ˚ = ulaṭ˚ = palaṭ˚ = Prk. पल्लट्ट = S. पर्यस्त, p.p.p. of परि+अस्; +nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To turn over; to turn back, to return; to retreat; — to go back (from), to be false (to), to play false
s.m. An almond; — a kind of sweetmeat.
s.f. = next, q.v.
s.m. A confection of almonds; any food in which almonds form a part.

ety: pl. of lauzīyat, fem. of lauzī, rel. n. fr. lauz
def: s.f. Sweetmeats in which almonds are mixed, almond-confections; — almond-shaped sweetmeats (made of curds, &c.).
s.f. The painted quail, Perdix chinensis.
s.m. A cutter; reaper, a labourer employed to cut the crops; — the painted quail, Perdix chinensis (see lāʼo, lawā).
s.m. Parched grain (syn. khīl).

ety: lāw(nā) = lā(nā)+ā = iā = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Bringer, fetcher (syn. lāne-wālā; cf. lewā, dewā)
s.m. Supplication, request; trick, fraud; — jest, joke; — adulation, flattery ( = lāba); — the game of tip-cat (syn. gullī-ḍanḍā).
s.m. The painted quail, Perdix chinensis.
s.m. = lāba, or lāwa, qq.v.
part.; a rustic or dialec. form of lāyā, perf. part. of lānā, q.v.

s.m. Cutting, reaping (of corn), mowing; plucking, gathering (of flowers, &c.); — anything cut off; wool; hair; a section, fragment, fraction, portion, piece, particle, bit, drop; — a minute division of time, the sixtieth of a twinkling, half a second, a moment, an instant; a larger division of time (thirty-six twinklings of the eye); — (in Arith.) the numerator (of a fraction); — loss, destruction; — a nutmeg; a clove; — the painted quail (Perdix chinensis)
subs: s.m. A very small particle, an atom.

def: s.m. pl. (of lāḥiq, or lāḥiqa), Dependeacies; appurtenances; — relations, kindred; followers, dependants, clients, domestics, servants.

ety: law˚ = lau (Prk. लउ), q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता + इका (तिका)
def: s.f. Tenderness, affection, fondness.

ety: law(nā)+aiyā or waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. Cutter, reaper, harvestman.
s.m. The painted quail, Perdix chinensis (see lāwā and lawā).
s.m. An implement for cutting, a sickle, a reaping-hook.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A cutter, reaper.

ety: lāwā + lutrā, qq.v., or lāwā = lagāwā = lagāʼū, q.v.
def: s.m. A tale-bearer, tell-tale, blab, mischief-maker, backbiter (a dialec. form is lagā-lutrā).

ety: -pan = Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Tale-bearing, tittle-tattle; backbiting.
s.m. (dialec.) A skirt.

ety: contrac. of lagāwan, fr. lagāwanā = lagānā, q.v.
def: s.f. Something taken with food as a relish; a relish.

r: lavaṇ, lavan, loṇ, lon, lūṇ, lūn

def: s.m. Salt; rock or fossil salt; sea. salt; — saltness; — the sea of salt water; — adj. Salt, &c. ( = lonā, q.v.)
subs: (˚ṇa+āk˚), s.m. A salt-mine; — (met.) a mine of grace or beauty
(˚ṇa+am˚), s.m. 'A mass of salt water'; the sea, the ocean
or lavaṇ-sindhu, s.m. The sea of salt-water, salt sea; sea, ocean
s.f. A salt-mine; — name of the district of Sāmbhar in Ajmere (celebrated for its fossil salt)
(˚ṇa+ut˚), s.m. 'Best salt'; river or rock salt; nitre
s.m. (dialec.), A seller of salt (cf. nān-bāʼī)
adj. Having an aversion to salt
(-meṅ), To apply salt and chillies (to); — to use highly seasoned language; to speak hyperbolically, or in lofty diffuse language.

def: s.m. Cutting, reaping; harvesting.
s.f. Light, lustre; beauty; — name of a river in Tirhut.
s.m. = lavāk, q.v.
= S لونامبو लवणाम्बु lavaṇāmbū, See s.v. lavaṇ, 'salt.'
= S لوناکر लवणाकर lavaṇākar, See s.v. lavaṇ, 'salt.'

ety: law˚, prob. = S. लव, rt. लू;+˚and = S. aff. अन्त्
def: adj. & s.m. Free, independent; — without a fixed residence; — ignorant, unlearned, foolish; — slothful, sluggish, lazy; — poor, scarce of provisions; — uxorious; — a soldier; a volunteer; an adventurer; — a libertine, a rake, a frequenter of taverns and stews, a gallant.
s.m. The clove-tree, Eugenia caryophillata; — a clove.
s.f. A coarse kind of custard-apple, Anona reticulata.

ety: Prk. लवणिअं = S. लवनीयं, rt. लू
def: s.f. Cutting (of crops), reaping; — wages in kind to reapers (in harvest time); — proceeds from land, rental, revenue; — a kind of song (syn. marahṭī)
s.f. Wages for cutting or reaping; wages in kind to reapers in harvest time.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating to salt; impregnated with salt; prepared or dressed with salt, salted; savoury; piquant; lovely, beautiful, graceful; — a salt-merchant; — a vessel holding salt; a salt-cellar.
s.f. A woman who deals in salt; — the wife of a salt-merchant; — a lovely woman.
s.m. Saltness; savouriness; piquancy; agreeableness; loveliness, beauty, charm, elegance, grace
subs: (fem. of lāvanya-vān), s.f. A lovely woman; — a proper name of women.

def: s.m. = lāgar, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = labār, q.v.
s.m. = lawar, q.v.
s.m. Flame (also written lawaṛ).

ety: prob. Prk. लवालओ; S. लप+कार+कः; but cf. labār
def: s.m. The young (of cattle); a calf (syn. lerū, leruʼā).

(pl. of lāzim, q.v.), = A لوازمات lawāzimāt (pl. of lawāzim), = P لوازمه lawāzima (for A. لوازمة lawāzimat, fem. of lawāzim), s.m. pl. Necessary things, necessaries, requisites; ingredients; appurtenances; baggage; accompaniments, consequences, inevitable results.

(for A. لوازمة lawāzimat, fem. of lawāzim), = A لوازم lawāzim (pl. of lāzim, q.v.), = A لوازمات lawāzimāt (pl. of lawāzim), s.m. pl. Necessary things, necessaries, requisites; ingredients; appurtenances; baggage; accompaniments, consequences, inevitable results.

(pl. of lawāzim), = A لوازم lawāzim (pl. of lāzim, q.v.), = P لوازمه lawāzima (for A. لوازمة lawāzimat, fem. of lawāzim), s.m. pl. Necessary things, necessaries, requisites; ingredients; appurtenances; baggage; accompaniments, consequences, inevitable results.

def: past conj. part. of lawnā, 'to cut,' q.v.

def: adv. (dialec.) Much, many, more, extremely, very.
adj. & s.m. Bringing, getting, procuring, giving, yielding; — bringer, &c. (used as last member of compounds).

s.m. A wolf (syn. bheṛiyā, bik.)
postp. (rustic, or dialec.) = liye, q.v.
= A اذات laẕẕāt, s.f. pl. of laẕẕat, q.v.
s.m. = lājaward, q.v.
adj. Firmly adhering; adhesive; hard, firm, solid; difficult.

ety: fr. lazaj, rt. لزج 'to be drawn out' (as anything viscous
def: adj. Tenacious, adhesive, viscous, sticky, clammy; stringy; ductile.

ety: act. part. of لزم 'to be necessary,' &c.
def: adj. Necessary, requisite; in dispensable; obligatory, incumbent; urgent, important; — suitable, proper, meet; — inseparably belonging or adhering (to); inseparable; adhering; inherent; (in Gram.) intransitive; neuter (e.g. feʻl-ě-lāzim); — s.m. A necessary or requisite thing; a necessary concomitant, an invariable (or inseparable) attendant
subs: or lāzim paṛnā (-ko), To be or become necessary (for); to follow of necessity; to be incumbent (on), to behove
v.t. To consider necessary, or right; to deem indispensable
v.t. To make (a thing) necessary (for, -par or -ūpar), to render incumbent or obligatory (on)
adj. Necessary; inseparable; incumbent, obligatory ( = lāzim); reciprocal; reciprocated; correlative; — s.m. Reciprocated (or intimate) friendship
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَازِم (lāzim), from Arabic لَازِم (lāzim).

ety: for A. لازمة lāzimat, fem. of lāzim
def: s.m. A necessary thing; a necessity, exigence; an obligation.
adj. Necessary; inevitable; — s.f. Anything that is necessary; what must be; a necessity.
adj. Sweet, delicious, luscious; pleasant, delightful; savoury, tasteful, nice.

ety: for A. لزوجة, v.n. fr. lazaj
def: s.f. Tenacity, adhesiveness, viscosity, stickiness, clamminess, &c.
s.f. Name of a hell; hell, hell-fire.
s.f. Pleasure, delight, enjoyment; sweetness, deliciousness; taste, flavour, relish, savour; — an aphrodisiac; an amorous philter (pl. laẕāʼiẕ and laẕẕāt)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَذَّت (lazzat), borrowed from Arabic لَذَّة (laḏḏa), from لَذَّ (laḏḏa). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi لذت (lẕt /⁠lazzat⁠/).

def: = A اذائذ laẕāʼiẕ, s.f. pl. of laẕẕat, q.v.

ety: conj. part. of lenā; Prk. लहिअ, or लेइअ; S. लब्ध्वा, rt. लभ्
def: part. Having taken, &c. (see lenā); — adv. With, together with (com. used as first member of comp. verbs, e.g.) le-uṛnā (-ko), To fly away with; to set off; to blaze up
subs: (-ko), To come with, to bring; to produce; to import; — le-ān-hārā, s.m. Bringer, producer
(-ko), To run away with, be off with, carry off, abduct
s.m. One who is in the habit of running off with (things); one who picks up and carries off without being taught
(-ko), To sit down with; to settle down with; to bring (another) down with (oneself)
(fr. le-pālnā), s.m. An adopted child, a foster-child
v.t. = le-pālnā (-ko), To adopt, rear, bring up, father
(-ko), To lie with, coire; — to involve (another) in (one's) disgrace
(-ko), To go away with, to take away; to carry, convey (to); to export; to refer (to); to carry off or away, bear off; to run away with; to win; to conquer, master
(-ko), To take along (or in company) with; to carry, convey, conduct, lead
adv. After all deductions or payments; in all, altogether
v.t. To down with; to fall on to (usually an intensive form of lenā, q.v.)
(-ko), To sink or be drowned with, to go down with
(-ko), To put by, lay by; to provide, to keep ready
(-ko), To earn, get, pick up; to cheat; to pilfer
v.t. To take away again, to take back (also intens. of lenā, q.v.)
(-ko), To die with; — to accuse falsely, to calumniate
(-ko), To come out with; to carry off or away.

ety: Prk. लहिए, or लइए = S. लब्धे
def: prep. From, beginning from; — till, to; inclusive (syn. loṅ).
intj. There! lo! come! well! I say! (syn. lo, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To use great exertion, to try hard; — to reprove, rebuke
or le-de-ke, adv. After great exertion, with much difficulty.

def: For this, and for other comp. verbs beginning with le which are not found below, see s.v. le.
s.m. = lewṛā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = regaṛ, q.v.
s.m. Licking, sipping tasting; — licker, sipper (used as last member of compounds).
s.m. Licking, tasting or lapping (with the tongue).
s.m. (dialec.) A flock (of goats, &c. = leṅḍhā, q.v.).
s.f. Paste ( = leʼī, the com. form)
s.m. Borax.

ety: Prk. लेहणअं; S. लेहनीयं, rt. लिह्
def: s.m. 'Food that is licked'; pasture; fodder; provender.
adj. (f. -ī), Filthy, dirty, impure.
s.m. (dialec.) = lohū, lahū, qq.v.

adj. & s.m. To be licked or licked up, capable of being licked; fit or proper to be licked or lapped; — food that is licked or sipped; spoon-diet, slops; a lambative; syrup; an electuary; — nectar, ambrosia.
s.m. Taker, receiver, &c. (see lenā).

ety: for lehī = les, q.v + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Paste; starch.

ety: Prk. लंजु, and लंजुडिआ; S. रज्जु+र+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Rope, cord, string, line.

ety: Prk. लंजु, and लंजुडिआ; S. रज्जु+र+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Rope, cord, string, line.

ety: Prk. लंजु, and लंजुडिआ; S. रज्जु+र+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Rope, cord, string, line.
contrac. of lekin, q.v. (gen. used in poetry, and with و wa prefixed).

ety: le = Prk. लहिअ or लेइअ = S. लब्ध्वा, rt. लभ्+kar = Prk. करिअ = S. कृत्वा, rt. कृ
def: adv. Having taken, &c. (see lenā); — with, along with, together with; keeping; inclusive (of); beginning (from, — e.g. āj-se lekar, 'beginning from to-day'
subs: from that time forward'; — use dekhne-ke ćhan-se lekar, 'from the moment of seeing him'; — ādi-se leke, 'and so forth').

def: part. & adv. = le-kar, q.v.
s.f. A writing; handwriting, signature, &c. (see lekhā).

s.f. Scratch, streak, line, stripe, stroke, furrow, groove; row; — writing, handwriting; signature; drawing lines, delineation, drawing, painting; a drawing, likeness, representation, figure, mark; an account (but in this sense the word is generally masc.; see next).

s.m. A written document; an account, a reckoning; a ledger ( = lekhā-bahī); — account, reckoning, regard, estimation; — state, condition, style, fashion (syn. ḥāl; e.g. ūṅće ćaṛhke dekhā to ghar ghar ek-hī lekhā, 'When I looked from a height the houses were all of one state' (or 'of one line or level'), 'All trees are of one height when you look down from the moon')
subs: s.m. Written documents, papers; — an account, a bill
(-kā), or lekhā pharćā karnā (-kā), To settle or close an account; to discharge a balance
s.m. Balancing of an account (see jamʻ-ḵẖarć); — an account, a reckoning
(-meṅ), To enter an account; to open an account (with)
To square accounts; to pay (one's) score
(-kā), To make the reckoning or computation (of), to compute, reckon
To take an account (from, -se, of a thing, -kā)
adv. In the estimation (of, -ke), to (e.g. andhe-ke lekhe jaisī rāt waisā din, 'to the blind man the night is as the day')

def: s.m. A writing; a letter, an epistle; a document; a bill.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A writer, scribe, copyist, clerk, amanuensis; an author; an accountant; — one who delineates or paints.
s.f. The business of a writer or scribe.

s.m. Scratching, scraping; — scarifying; — writing, copying; a writing (in this sense, also fem. in Hindī), scripture; — a kind of birch (the bark of which is used for writing on in Naipāl and Upper India)
subs: s.f. Method of writing (i.e. by figures or letters; whereas likhan simply means 'writing').

def: s.f. = lekhanī, q.v.
s.f. An instrument for writing, a style; a writing-reed, reed-pen; a pen.
adv. See s.v. lekhā.

ety: lekh˚ = S. लेखय(ति), caus. of rt. लिख्; + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To scratch, scrape; to dig, delve; to scoop; to engrave.
v.t. (dialec.) = lakhnā, q.v.
adj. To be written or transcribed; proper or necessary to be written; — s.m. A writing; document; manuseript; letter, epistle
subs: s.m. A written paper, a writing, letter, document
s.m. Writing-place, office; a country house.

def: conj. But, but still, on the other hand, however, notwithstanding, nevertheless, yet.
s.m. 1˚ = lekhan, q.v.; — 2˚ = lakshaṇ, q.v.

ety: prob. akin to S. लेलाय 'to move to and fro,' 'to skip'
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A lamb.

ety: leliy˚ = lelī, fem. of lelā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v. intr. To bleat.
adj. & s.m. = lahloṭ, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lepan, q.v.
v.n. See s.v. le, conj. part
s.m. Taking, receiving, &c.
subs: (dialec. lain-dain), s.m. 'Taking and giving'; buying and selling; borrowing and lending; traffic, trade, barter; business, transaction, dealing; intercourse, communication
To stop dealing (with, -se; or in, -kā); to close a business or traffic
To carry on a business, to transact; to trade (in, kā); to deal (with, -se).

ety: le˚ = Prk. ले(इ) = लह(इ) = S. लभ(ते), rt. लभ्;+mī = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To take, accept, receive; to hold, grasp, lay hold of; to get, obtain, acquire; to reach; to buy, purchase; to borrow; — to absorb, consume; to take possession of, to appropriate; to seize, capture; to win; to overcome, master, conquer; to browbeat; to extort; — to take to task, to chide, rebuke, reprove; — to pick, pluck out (hair); to cut, pare (the nails); — (as a gerund), s.m. Taking, receiving, &c. ( = len); — money to be realized, outstanding debt (the verb lenā is frequently annexed to the base of another verb to form intensives, and it becomes neuter itself when the leading verb is such; it is also occasionally used to form nominal verbs, e.g. kām lenā (-se), 'to take work' (from), 'to make use' (of); — mol lenā, 'to purchase')
subs: v.t. To master completely; to thoroughly overpower or subdue; — to take sharply to task, to rebuke severely; to rate, scold, &c.; to disgrace, to humiliate
v.t. To take, receive, &c. ( = lenā); to take away (from, -se); to conquer or master completely; to take possession of, to seize upon; to take by force; to extort
'Nor one to receive nor two to give'; having nothing to gain or lose
(-ko), To come out (or forth) to receive (a person)
'To have to pay instead of receiving'; to be drawn into a difficulty; to be or become a loser (by); — to be on the point of death, to be past all hope.

def: s.f. Excrement, filth; orbicular dung (as of camels, goats, &c.); a lump of hardened fæces or scybala ( = leṅḍī).
s.m. See s.v. len.
s.m. Smut, mildew (syn. geruʼī); — a flock (of goats or sheep), a pack (of dogs).
s.f. Excrement, dung (of sheep, or goats, &c.); a lump or nodule of dung (see leṅḍ); — (fig.) a cur, a pariah dog.
adj. & s.m. Impotent; cowardly; — an impotent fellow; a coward.
s.m. An impotent fellow; a coward; — a son by a former husband.
s.f. Lot, fate, fortune ( = lahnā, q.v.).
s.f. = leṅḍ, and leṅḍī, qq.v.
s.f. = leṅḍ, and leṅḍī, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of reṇu, q.v.

s.m. Smearing, daubing, anointing, plastering; — salve, unguent, ointment, plaster, liniment; mortar, chunam, whitewash; — the wipings of the hands after offering funeral oblations to three ancestors (these wipings being considered as an oblation to paternal ancestors in the fourth, fifth, and sixth degrees); — impurity, dirt; — a spot, stain, pollution; — food, victuals
subs: (-ko), lep ćaṛhānā, or lep lagānā (-meṅ), To plaster; to apply a plaster or ointment, &c. (to).

def: s.m. A bricklayer; a plasterer (syn. lepī).
adj. Smearing (with), plastering; covering (with); — s.m. A plasterer; a bricklayer.
s.m. Plastering, spreading (mortar or ointment), smearing, &c.
s.m. A calf (cf. lelā.)
s.m. A calf (cf. lelā.)

ety: Prk. लेसं; S. लेशं, rt. लिश्
def: s.m. Smallness, littleness; — a small part or portion, a particle, little bit, slight trace; mark, sign, effect (cf. S. laksham); — adj. Small, little.
s.m. Plastering; plaster; viscosity, glutinousness, stickiness, adhesiveness; — anything sticky, paste (syn. lehī, leʼī); glue; gum; pitch
s.m. = ليس les, 'littleness,' &c., q.v.
s.f. The act of plastering, or smearing, or daubing (with mud, or dung, &c.).

ety: les˚ = Prk. सिलेस(इ) = S. श्लेषय(ति), caus. fr. rt. श्लिष्; cf. ḍhīlā
def: v.t. To cause to adhere; to plaster (with mud and dung), to daub over, to smear, to spread; — to cause to burn, to kindle; to foment (a quarrel).

ety: prob. Prk. लित्तो = S. लिप्तः, p.p.p. fr. rt. लिप्
def: s.m. Plaster; — paste; batter.
s.m. = ليت let, q.v.
v.t. To besmear; to draggle; to clog; to fatigue, weary.

ety: leṭ˚ = S. लेट्य(ति), fr. लेट्य; or leṭnā = loṭnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To lie down, to lie prostrate; to repose, rest; — to fall (at the feet of), to prostrate ooneself; — to submit, yield; — to become thin or sloppy; to melt (these meanings point to S. ली as the probable source of the word)

ety: Prk. लेवो; S. लेप, rt. लिप्
def: s.m. Plastor; old plaster which comes off a wall
subs: (-par), Plaster to be put on; to be plastered; (fig.) flesh to be put on, to grow fat.

def: s.m. Plaster ( = leʼo); the ashes or earth which is spread on the outside of a new cooking-vessel; — an udder.

ety: le(nā)+wā = awā or iwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. One who takes, taker, &c. (see lenā); — taking, receiving, &c. ( = len, lenā)
s.m. = liwaiyā, and lewā, qq.v.
v.t. (rustic) = liwānā, q.v.

ety: lewā, q.v.+r = Prk. डो = S. रः
def: s.m. Plaster ( = lewā); mud (for plastering a wall, &c.).

ety: lewā, q.v.+s = sar = sari = sariʼa = Prk. सरिओ = S. सदृशः
def: s.m. Plaster; chunam; stucco.

ety: lew, q.v. + aṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र+कः
def: s.m. = lewār, q.v.
s.f. A bow with an iron chain instead of a string, with which athletes exercise themselves; a weak bow for practising with (in Pers. also luzūm).
adj. = lais, q.v.
s.m. Stickiness, &c. ( = les, q.v.).
v.t. To besmear, plaster, &c. ( = lesnā, q.v.).
s.f. = līr, q.v.
s.f. = līrī = līr, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lohā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lohār, q.v.
s.m. Land that is annually flooded.
adj. & s.m. Wise, prudent, intelligent; — the wise, or prudent, &c.
s.m. Rheum (of the eyes).

ety: v.n. fr. لبس 'to put on a garment,' &c.
def: s.m. Garment, vesture, raiment, robe, apparel, clothes, dress, attire, habit; appearance; guise; a veil; — forgery
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لِباس, from Arabic لِبَاس (libās). Compare Punjabi ਲਿਬਾਸ (libās) / لِباس (libās), Gujarati લિબાસ (libās), લેબાસ (lebās).

def: adj. Veiled; apparent; formal; — fictitious, pretended, false, forged, counterfeit, sham.
s.m. A slap, buffet.
s.m.(?) Felt-cloth; any kind of carpeting or saddle-cloths made of felt; coarse covering made of wool or hair.
adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, clammy; slimy, viscous, glutinous (syn. laslasā); — pliable, flexible; — s.m. The pancreas.
adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, clammy; slimy, viscous, glutinous (syn. laslasā); — pliable, flexible; — s.m. The pancreas.
s.f. The sear spring of a gun-lock.
s.m. A brick or tile; — a mural quadrant.

ety: Prk. लिच्छडो; S. लिप्स+रः, rt. लभ्
def: adj. & s.m. Stingy, grasping, niggardly, miserly, avaricious; — a miser, niggard; one who is slow in paying his debts, a bad paymaster

ety: līćaṛ, q.v. + pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic. त्वनं)
def: s.m. Stinginess, miserliness; slowness in paying one's debts.
s.f. = līćī, q.v.
s.f. The fruit Scytalia litchi (first introduced from China).

ety: līć, prob. = S. लीय(ते), rt. ली
def: v.n. To cower, submit, succumb, subside.
s.m. = līćī, q.v.

ety: prob. Prk. लित्ता; S. लिप्ता, rt. लिप्; lit. 'what is smeared or plastered over'
def: s.f. Dung (of horses, mules, asses, and elephants)

ety: for A. لفافة lifāfat, v.n. fr. لفّ; see laff
def: s.m. A wrapper, outward case; enclosure; cover of a letter, an envelope; — (fig.) gilding; — whitewash; a gloss; — outward show, ostentation; — anything frail; gingerbread work
subs: To make an envelope, &c.; — to make a show or display
v.t. To wrap up, to enclose; to put (a letter) into an envelope; — to gild (glass, &c.)
(-kā), A covering or wrapper, &c. to be opened or removed; (a person or thing) to be laid bare or exposed

def: adj. Having a gaudy outer cover; showy, ostentatious.
adv. To the end (of); inclusive.
s.m. A coverlet, quilt, counterpane; a quilted upper garment; — (met.) a wife.

ety: prob. les(nā)+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका; cf. lehī
def: s.f. A kind of paste or glue (made from tamarind stones bolled and pounded to a jelly) used by bookbinders.

ety: S. लिह (rt. लिह्), or लेप (rt. लिप्)+aiṅḍī = Prk. अड़िआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Irrigation of land by throwing up water from a lower level (by means of a kind of basket worked by two persons).
adj. Vile, base, mean, contemptible; — a shabby fellow; a lick-penny; a bad paymaster.

ety: inf. n. of لاحظ; iii of لحظ 'to look through half-shut eyes'
def: s.m. Attentive observation, attention, notice, look, glance, view; regard, consideration, respect, sense, deference; importance, weight; shame; respect, reference, relation, advertence
subs: To abandon shame, to become shameless
(-ko, or -kā), To behold, look at, regard, notice, observe, consider, have regard to; to mind, heed, attend to; to pay respect to, to defer to; to be partial to, to favour; to refer to, to advert to
To pay no attention (to), to disregard, to turn away (from); — to lose all sense of shame, to be shameless or immodest
adj. (f. -ī), Having a sense of shame; modest
adv. In consideration (of, -ke), considering; in respect (of), with regard (to), with reference or advertence (to)
adj. Inattentive, heedless; shameless; — be-liḥāz̤ī, s.f. Heedlessness; shamelessness.

def: adv. For this reason; because of this; therefore, consequently.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لِهٰذا (lihāzā), from Arabic لِهٰذَا (lihāḏā).

def: s.f. Beard; mustachio; whisker
subs: s.f. The plant goat's beard, Hypoestes (?).

ety: lījh = S. श्लिष्य(ति), rt. श्लिष् + ī = S. इका
def: s.f. Dregs (of a dye, or of anything that has been chewed, &c.); sediment; refuse, remains (cf. lijhṛī, and lijlijā).

ety: prob. fr. S. श्लिष्य˚+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका; cf. lījhī
def: s.f. After-birth, placenta.
adj. (f. -ī), Clammy, sticky, viscous, glutinous; — soft, flaccid, flabby; flexible.

ety: Prk. लिक्खा, लीहा, लेहा; S. लेखा, rt. लिख्
def: s.f. Mark (of a cart-wheel), wheel-track, rut, furrow, groove, path, track, trace; — established practice, custom, usage, order, rule (e.g. bandhī līk); — stain, stigma, ignominy, disgrace
subs: (-kī), To follow the beaten path, to move in a groove, &c. ( = lakīr pīṭnā, līk-līk ćalnā); — to cry over spilt milk
To deviate from the common track or course
To go or move in a rut or groove; to follow the beaten path; to adhere to old-established customs or practices (syn. lakīr pīṭnā).

def: s.f. = ليکهہ līkh, 'a nit,' q.v.
s.f. Meeting, encountering, seeing (a person; cf. mulāqāt); fighting (with); (met. perhaps for liqāʼuʼl-lāh) death; — (in Pers.) face, form, visage, countenance (used as last member of compounds)
subs: adj. With a face like the moon, moon-faced.

def: s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = līk, q.v.; — 2˚ = lekhā, q.v.
